Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Discovery (Read/Lookup Actions):
Search emails to find the forwarded message from David regarding Pam's threat and the Friday deadline using the search_emails tool (query: "Pam" or "NorthWind" or "David").
Search the operations Slack channels and Linear ticketing system to uncover the exact details of the server and piano disasters using conversations_search_messages and linear_list_issues (query: "piano", "server", or "NorthWind").
Search emails or Slack for any internal intel regarding the competitor using search_emails or conversations_search_messages (query: "PathFinder Logistics").
Search Linear issues assigned to Catalina to find the started notes for the missed recovery plan using linear_list_issues (status/assignee filters).
Search Slack or emails to track down the pre-approved retention discount mentioned by David using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "discount", "retention", or "David").
Look up NorthWind's internal ID in the CRM using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "NorthWind").
Verify if Pam Kowalski and Victor Huang are listed as active contacts for NorthWind using crm_search_contacts (query: "Pam" or "Victor Huang").
Look up David Chen's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen") to find his email address (david.chen@moveops.com).
Execution (Write Actions):
Update NorthWind's CRM status to reflect the severe churn risk using crm_update_deal_stage (setting dealstage to "at_risk" or similar churn/risk status).
Create CRM contacts for Pam Kowalski and/or Victor Huang using crm_create_contact if the previous search revealed they were missing from the database.
Schedule a 30-minute review meeting with David Chen for tomorrow morning using calendar_add_calendar_event (parameters: attendees: david.chen@moveops.com, time: morning block for April 27, 2026).
Send the drafted retention proposal to David Chen for his review before the meeting using send_email or conversations_add_message (recipient: david.chen@moveops.com). The content must incorporate the server/piano failure details, the recovery plan notes, and David's pre-approved discount numbers.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
Discovery (Read/Lookup Actions):
Search emails to find the forwarded message from David regarding Pam's threat and the Friday deadline using the search_emails tool (query: "Pam" or "NorthWind" or "David").
Search the operations Slack channels and Linear ticketing system to uncover the exact details of the server and piano disasters using conversations_search_messages and linear_list_issues (query: "piano", "server", or "NorthWind").
Search emails or Slack for any internal intel regarding the competitor using search_emails or conversations_search_messages (query: "PathFinder Logistics").
Search Linear issues assigned to Catalina to find the started notes for the missed recovery plan using linear_list_issues (status/assignee filters).
Search Slack or emails to track down the pre-approved retention discount mentioned by David using conversations_search_messages or search_emails (query: "discount", "retention", or "David").
Look up NorthWind's internal ID in the CRM using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals (query: "NorthWind").
Verify if Pam Kowalski and Victor Huang are listed as active contacts for NorthWind using crm_search_contacts (query: "Pam" or "Victor Huang").
Look up David Chen's contact info using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen") to find his email address (david.chen@moveops.com).
Execution (Write Actions):
Update NorthWind's CRM status to reflect the severe churn risk using crm_update_deal_stage (setting dealstage to "at_risk" or similar churn/risk status).
Create CRM contacts for Pam Kowalski and/or Victor Huang using crm_create_contact if the previous search revealed they were missing from the database.
Schedule a 30-minute review meeting with David Chen for tomorrow morning using calendar_add_calendar_event (parameters: attendees: david.chen@moveops.com, time: morning block for April 27, 2026).
Send the drafted retention proposal to David Chen for his review before the meeting using send_email or conversations_add_message (recipient: david.chen@moveops.com). The content must incorporate the server/piano failure details, the recovery plan notes, and David's pre-approved discount numbers.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The NorthWind Technologies deal was updated in the CRM (via crm_update_deal_stage).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2'. The first attempt failed due to invalid stage name, but a second call succeeded with dealstage 'presentationscheduled', confirmed by the tool result showing the updated deal.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_update_deal_stage twice — once for 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2' and once for 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion'. Both calls succeeded (confirmed by the tool results showing the updated records).
✓Run #3The trajectory shows two calls to crm_update_deal_stage with id 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2'. The first failed due to capitalization, the second succeeded using 'decisionmakerboughtin'. The tool was clearly used.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_update_deal_stage for both deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 and deal_northwind_denver_expansion successfully (the second attempt using 'presentationscheduled' returned successful results as confirmed in the output files).
✓Run #5The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2'. The first attempt with 'atRisk' failed (invalid stage), but a second call succeeded with 'presentationscheduled', confirmed by the successful result in tool_call_result_19.json.
✗Run #6The agent called crm_update_deal_stage with id 'deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2' and dealstage 'decisionMakerBoughtIn', but the tool returned an error: 'Invalid deal stage: decisionMakerBoughtIn. Valid stages: [appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, presentationscheduled, decisionmakerboughtin, contractsent, closedwon, closedlost]'. The call failed due to incorrect casing (capital 'M' and 'B' in 'decisionMakerBoughtIn' vs lowercase 'decisionmakerboughtin'). The deal stage was never actually updated.
2 The updated CRM deal stage reflects a severe risk, churn risk, or negotiation status (or similar).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗—✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent updated the deal stage to 'presentationscheduled', which does not reflect a churn risk or at-risk status. None of the valid stages (appointmentscheduled, qualifiedtobuy, presentationscheduled, decisionmakerboughtin, contractsent, closedwon, closedlost) directly represent churn risk or negotiation status. The agent did create a churn-risk engagement NOTE, but the deal stage itself does not reflect the urgency. 'presentationscheduled' is actually a forward-progress stage, not a risk/at-risk indicator.
✗Run #2The agent first tried 'Churn Risk - Active Retention' (rejected as invalid). It then fell back to 'closedlost', which marks the deal as already closed/lost rather than reflecting an active churn risk or negotiation status. 'closedlost' misrepresents the situation — the team is actively trying to retain NorthWind, not writing off the account. None of the valid stages used map to 'churn risk' or 'negotiation'.
✗Run #3The deal stage was updated to 'decisionmakerboughtin' (Decision Maker Bought In), which is a positive/forward sales stage, not one that reflects severe churn risk or at-risk status. This stage actually implies a positive outcome and fails to convey the urgency of the churn threat.
✗Run #4The agent first tried 'Churn Risk - Active Save' but got an error (invalid stage). It then fell back to 'presentationscheduled', which does not reflect a churn risk or severe risk status. While the agent added a detailed churn risk engagement note, the actual deal stage update does not convey the urgency required by the criterion.
—Run #5The agent tried to set the stage to 'atRisk' (which directly reflects churn risk) but it was invalid. It fell back to 'presentationscheduled', which is a generic pipeline stage rather than one specifically reflecting churn/severe risk. However, the agent supplemented this with a detailed CHURN RISK engagement note explaining the urgency. The dealstage field itself does not clearly convey churn risk.
✗Run #6The crm_update_deal_stage call failed entirely (API error due to invalid stage value). Even if it had succeeded, the intended stage 'decisionMakerBoughtIn' does not reflect churn risk, severe risk, or negotiation status — it refers to a sales pipeline milestone, not a retention/churn flag.

outcome

outcome

Both incidents are described in detail.
✓Run #2The message sent to David explicitly details both the server incident (Raj Patel, SentinelChain crew arriving 2 hours early, unauthorized loading, warehouse storage) and the piano damage incident (Emilia Cruz, Steinway Model B scratched by KeyMove crew, missed callback, $1,200 claim rider).

✓Run #6The Slack message to David explicitly details both disasters: the Raj Patel server lab incident (SentinelChain crew loading before IT escort, overnight warehouse storage, chain-of-custody breach) and the Emilia Cruz piano damage (Steinway Model B scratched, site survey failure, $1,200 insurance claim).

outcome

8 The message sent to David incorporates the pre-approved retention discount
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The message to David includes commercial terms referencing 'Alejandro's draft model + your guidance from the Vectral playbook': 10% Preferred Partner loyalty discount, absorption of piano restoration costs, Net 45 payment terms, and revised invoice from $14,700 to $13,230.
✓Run #2The message to David explicitly includes a '10% loyalty discount on all future relocation services' under the 'Preferred Partner Retention Program' section, along with $1,200 claim credit and Net 45 payment terms.
✓Run #3The message to David explicitly includes commercial consideration details: 10% discount on relocation services, $1,470 immediate credit, $1,200 chain-of-custody fee waiver, Net 45 terms, and a total immediate credit of $2,670 — all framed as using 'Alejandro's framework + Marcus's Vectral precedent.'
✓Run #4The message includes a detailed commercial consideration section with a 10% loyalty discount, surcharge waivers, Net 45 terms, and a revised invoice total ($14,700 → $12,600), referencing the Vectral Preferred Partner framework and Alejandro's retention pricing model.
✓Run #5The message explicitly includes the pre-approved retention discount in Section IV: 10% loyalty discount (mirroring the Vectral/Elena precedent, stays above 25% margin floor), $1,200 chain-of-custody waiver, and $500 lease negotiation waiver, resulting in a net adjusted invoice of $11,730.
✓Run #6The message sent to David includes a detailed commercial consideration section with the retention discount package: 10% fee reduction on all relocation services for 12 months, $3,000 service credit, Net 45 payment terms, and waived chain-of-custody and lease negotiation fees (~$6,170 total value).
